interpretations.title: Results and Interpretations
rows: May need FNA if ≥1 cm or high-risk features
>1 cm, round shape, loss of fatty hilum

faqs.question: Is the scan painful?
faqs.answer: No—only light pressure on the neck.

faqs.question: How soon can I eat or drink?
Will I get a copy of the images?
faqs.answer: Yes, a **digital copy** is emailed and stored on the Cadabams mobile app.

faqs.question: What if a nodule is found?
faqs.answer: Your report will include **TI-RADS score**; our radiologists will guide you to the next step—often a simple FNA.

faqs.question: Cost and insurance coverage?
faqs.answer: Transparent pricing starts at ₹1,200. We support **cashless insurance and corporate tie-ups**.

seo.title: Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan: Safe & Quick at Cadabams Diagnostics
seo.description: THYROID/NECK ULTRASOUND SCAN: Safest way to detect lumps, nodules, and goitre with real-time, no-radiation imaging and instant reports at Cadabams.

test.markdown:
## Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan Overview

### What the Scan Is and Why It Matters
- \*\*Real-time imaging\*\*: Sound waves bounce off tissues to build live pictures on a screen.
- \*\*No radiation\*\*: Safe for babies, pregnant women, and the elderly.
- \*\*Early detection\*\*: Spots nodules as small as 2 mm—years before they can be felt.
### How It Compares to Other Imaging Options
| Imaging Type | Uses | Radiation | Imaging Depth | Typical Duration |
|--------------|------|-----------|---------------|------------------|
| Ultrasound | Sound waves | None | Superficial neck | 15-20 min |
| CT | X-rays | Yes | Deep chest/abdomen | 10-30 min |
| MRI | Magnets | None | Brain/spine | 30-60 min |
---
## What Is a Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan?
### Definition and Technology Used
High-frequency sound waves (3–15 MHz) are sent into the neck by a handheld probe. Echoes return and are translated into a \*\*real-time image\*\* on the monitor.
### Difference Between Thyroid and Full Neck Ultrasound
- \*\*Thyroid-focused\*\*: Examines only the thyroid gland (both lobes & isthmus).
- \*\*Full neck ultrasound\*\*: Includes salivary glands, lymph nodes, carotid arteries, and soft tissues.
---
## Types of Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan
| Type | Purpose | Typical Use Case |
|------|---------|------------------|
| \*\*Grayscale ultrasound\*\* | Anatomy & texture | Routine screening |
| \*\*Doppler ultrasound\*\* | Blood flow in nodules | Differentiate cancer risk |
| \*\*Contrast-enhanced ultrasound\*\* | Vascularity mapping | Selected complex cases |
---

## When and Who Needs to Take a Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan?

### Symptoms That Trigger Referral
- Palpable \*\*lumps\*\* or swelling
- Persistent \*\*hoarseness\*\* or trouble swallowing
- Visible \*\*neck asymmetry\*\* when looking in the mirror
### High-Risk Groups
- Family history of \*\*thyroid cancer\*\*
- \*\*Abnormal TSH, T3, T4\*\* on blood tests
- History of \*\*thyroiditis\*\* or goitre
### Routine Screening Scenarios
- Annual follow-up for known nodules
- Pre-surgery mapping or post-surgery monitoring
---

## Risks & Limitations of the Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*No radiation exposure\*\* – safe for all ages and stages of pregnancy.
- \*\*Operator-dependency\*\* – image quality hinges on the sonographer’s skill; at Cadabams Diagnostics, every scan is done by certified specialists.
- \*\*Limitations\*\* – may miss \*\*microscopic lesions <2 mm\*\* or deep-seated parathyroid glands.
---

## List of Parameters Considered During the Scan

- \*\*Thyroid size & volume\*\* – >18 ml in women or >25 ml in men may signal goitre.
- \*\*Nodule characteristics\*\* – size, shape (round vs taller-than-wide), margins, calcifications.
- \*\*Cervical lymph nodes\*\* – size, cortex thickness, loss of fatty hilum.
- \*\*Vascularity patterns\*\* – peripheral vs central blood flow.
---

## Reasons for Taking a Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan

- Investigate \*\*palpable neck lumps\*\* felt by you or your doctor.
- Monitor \*\*known nodules\*\* every 6–12 months for growth.
- Guide \*\*fine-needle aspiration (FNA)\*\* biopsy with real-time needle tracking.
- Evaluate underlying causes of \*\*hyperthyroidism or hypothyroidism\*\*.
---

## Benefits of Taking the Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Quick\*\*: 15–20 minutes door-to-door.
- \*\*Painless\*\*: Only gentle pressure from the probe.
- \*\*Cost-effective\*\*: Up to 50 % cheaper than CT/MRI.
- \*\*Zero downtime\*\*: Resume work or drive immediately.
---

## Illnesses Diagnosed with a Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Thyroid nodules\*\* – distinguish benign colloid nodules from suspicious solid lesions.
- \*\*Goitre\*\* – diffuse or multinodular enlargement.
- \*\*Thyroiditis\*\* – Hashimoto’s shows heterogeneous “pseudonodular” pattern.
- \*\*Neck cysts or abscesses\*\* – anechoic (black) fluid-filled structures.
- \*\*Enlarged lymph nodes\*\* – reactive vs malignant signs.
---

## Preparing for Your Thyroid/Neck Ultrasound Scan

- \*\*Clothing\*\*: Wear a loose collar or V-neck; avoid turtlenecks.
- \*\*Jewellery\*\*: Remove necklaces and scarves before entering the scan room.
- \*\*Fasting\*\*: Not required—eat and drink normally.
---
## Pre-Requisites
- \*\*Valid doctor referral\*\* or prescription.
- \*\*Previous thyroid reports\*\* (blood tests, prior scans) if available.
- \*\*Patient ID\*\* and updated contact details for digital report delivery.
---
## Best Time to Take the Scan
- \*\*Any time of day\*\* – the thyroid does not fluctuate with meals or hormones.
- \*\*Preferably before\*\* invasive tests like FNA or surgery to plan next steps.
---
## Eligibility
- \*\*All ages\*\*: Newborns with congenital hypothyroidism to elderly with multinodular goitre.
- \*\*Pregnancy & breastfeeding\*\*: 100 % safe—no radiation or contrast needed.
---
## Procedure Step-by-Step
1. \*\*Check-in & consent\*\* at Cadabams Diagnostics reception.
2. \*\*Positioning\*\*: Lie on your back with a small pillow under your shoulders to extend the neck.
3. \*\*Gel application\*\*: Warm, water-based gel ensures smooth probe contact.
4. \*\*Scanning\*\*: The sonographer moves the transducer across your neck for 10–15 minutes.
5. \*\*Wipe-off & report\*\*: Gel is removed; preliminary findings handed to you instantly.
---
## Caution Before the Test
- \*\*Inform staff\*\* if you had \*\*recent neck surgery or biopsy\*\*—scar tissue may need special views.
- Report any \*\*contrast allergies\*\* if a contrast-enhanced study is planned (rarely required).
---
